name: Pyre (Meta's Python Type Checker)

performance.notes: Incremental server mode; fast after initial analysis

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's incremental Python type checker using OCaml backend. pyre init → initialize .pyre_configuration. pyre check → one-shot check. pyre → start incremental server. pyre rage → diagnostics. .pyre_configuration: { 'source_directories': ['.'], 'search_path': ['path/to/stubs'], 'python_version': '3.11' }. Pysa: static analysis for security (taint analysis). pyre analyze → run Pysa. Pysa rules: TaintSource, TaintSink, TaintInTaintOut. Models: .pysa files with sanitizers and rules. Incremental analysis makes it fast for large codebases. LSP server for IDE integration. Primarily used at Meta scale. Linux primarily.

gotchas: Much harder to set up than mypy or pyright
Linux preferred — macOS works but less tested
OCaml runtime required (installed via pip but unusual dep)
Pysa (security analysis) has very steep learning curve
best_when: Very large Python codebases needing incremental type checking
Security taint analysis via Pysa (unique capability)
When Pysa's data flow security analysis is required
avoid_when: Standard Python type checking (mypy or pyright is easier)
Small-medium projects (setup cost not worth it)
summary: Pyre is Meta's incremental Python type checker with Pysa security taint analysis. pyre init; pyre check or pyre (incremental server). Pysa: pyre analyze for data flow security (SQL injection, XSS taint tracking). .pyre_configuration + .pysa model files. MIT by Meta. OCaml backend. Complex setup vs mypy. Best at Meta scale or when Pysa's unique taint analysis is needed. mypy or pyright for standard type checking.
